_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Federal House batch: 2 NJ incumbents (Conaway NJ-03, Pou NJ-09) + 3 lost-primary 2026 candidates (Williams NE-02, Cohen NJ-12, Vaingankar NJ-12). Evidence-first; every FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Three candidacy-currency fixes (all LOST primaries -> status:lost).

herb-conaway.notes_set: Freshman D (sworn Jan 2025, succeeded Andy Kim), physician (MD/JD), ~26 yrs prior in NJ Assembly (Health Chair). Cosponsor H.R.12 Women's Health Protection Act (codify abortion) and H.R.15 Equality Act; campaign backs codifying Roe. As NJ Assembly Health Chair authored bill to ELIMINATE the religious exemption from school vaccine mandates, called parental opt-out a 'recipe for disaster' (anti-conscience-exemption, pro-mandate). Voted NO on Laken Riley Act ('to be snatched and detained without a hearing and deported is un-American'). Took AIPAC-affiliated AIEF-funded Israel trip Aug 2025. No conservative cross-party deviation found; no NRA/Heritage/SBA entries (freshman). Cells without evidence left null.

0.note: Campaign backs codifying Roe; cosponsor H.R.12 Women's Health Protection Act (abortion access). Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Cosponsors abortion-access codification; opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Cosponsor H.R.15 Equality Act; does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsor; supports same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Equality Act codifies gender-identity protections; rejects biological-sex immutability framing.

4.note: Equality Act actively expands LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: As NJ Assembly Health Chair authored/championed elimination of the religious exemption from school vaccine mandates; opposed conscience opt-outs.
Voted NO on Laken Riley Act; on record calling detention/deportation without a hearing 'un-American.' Opposes mandatory deportation.

3.note: Accepted AIPAC-affiliated (American Israel Education Foundation) funded Israel trip, Aug 2025.

0.note: Pro-mandate physician; led ending religious vaccine exemptions and opposed parental opt-outs (contrary to informed-consent / no-mandate position).

nellie-pou.notes_set: D (sworn Jan 2025, succeeded the late Bill Pascrell), decades prior in NJ Legislature. Progressive Caucus member; Heritage Action lifetime score 0%. EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsed/backed (reproductive-rights 'champion'); Garden State Equality endorsed (pro-same-sex-marriage / SOGI). Voted NO on the SAVE Act (citizenship voter-ID; 'solution in search of a problem'), NO on the Laken Riley Act (both votes) and related immigration-enforcement bills, NO on H.R.1. Anti-NRA; introduced the Crime Gun Tracing Modernization Act. Took AIPAC-affiliated AIEF-funded Israel trip Aug 2025. No conservative cross-party deviation found. Cells without evidence left null.

0.note: EMILY's List 'champion for reproductive rights'; Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsed. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Action Fund (abortion-industry PAC support).

0.note: Documented proponent of same-sex marriage; Garden State Equality endorsed. Does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

2.note: Supported adding sexual-orientation/gender-identity to anti-discrimination law; LGBTQ-advocacy endorsed.

4.note: Garden State Equality (LGBTQ advocacy) endorsement; supports LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: Voted NO on the SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter ID), calling it 'a solution in search of a problem.'
Voted NO on the Laken Riley Act (both H.R.29 and S.5) and related immigration-enforcement bills.
Introduced gun-control legislation (Crime Gun Tracing Modernization Act) and publicly criticized the NRA; pro-gun-regulation.

3.note: Accepted AIPAC-affiliated (AIEF) funded Israel trip, Aug 2025.

set.office: U.S. House candidate (NE-02, 2026) - LOST Democratic primary

melanie-williams-ne-02.notes_set: LOST the May 12, 2026 NE-02 Democratic primary, finishing last of six (~4%); Denise Powell won the nomination. Self-identified 'Democratic Socialist.' Documented platform (own campaign site / BallotReady): 'Abortion is healthcare,' codify Roe; LGBTQ champion / Equality Act / protect trans youth; Abolish ICE + pathway to citizenship; Medicare for All, Green New Deal, wealth tax, cancel student debt, reparations; 'Free Palestine,' end U.S. military aid to Israel. No school-choice, religious-liberty, election, guns, or health-freedom positions located. No formal single-issue org endorsement found. Status fix is the primary win here; positions scored from her own cited statements, rest null.

0.note: 'Abortion is healthcare'; vows to codify Roe and protect contraception. Does not affirm personhood from conception.
Self-described champion for LGBTQ rights; backs the Equality Act. Does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage via Equality Act advocacy.

2.note: Advocates 'protecting trans youth from anti-transgender legislation'; affirms transgender ideology.

4.note: Backs Equality Act / promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: Supports 'Abolish ICE' and a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants; opposes mandatory deportation.

set.office: U.S. House candidate (NJ-12, 2026) - LOST Democratic primary

brad-cohen.notes_set: LOST the June 2, 2026 NJ-12 Democratic primary, finishing 2nd (15.8%) in a 13-way field; Adam Hamawy won (seat open after Bonnie Watson Coleman's retirement). Mayor of East Brunswick, OBGYN. Documented positions (Daily Princetonian / WHYY voter guide): pro-choice in own words ('women will die'); pro-gun-control (universal background checks, assault-weapons ban, red-flag laws); anti-aggressive immigration enforcement ('un-American... racial,' but rejects 'abolish ICE'); pro-Israel. No LGBTQ, school-choice, religious-liberty, or health-freedom positions located. Campaign site taken down post-loss. Status fix is the primary win; positions from cited statements, rest null.

0.note: OBGYN; warned of complications from restricting abortion ('women will die'); pro-abortion-rights. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Called current immigration enforcement 'so un-American, and it's racial'; opposes mandatory deportation regime.
Supports universal background checks, an assault-weapons ban, and red-flag laws; pro-gun-control.

set.office: U.S. House candidate (NJ-12, 2026) - LOST Democratic primary

jay-vaingankar.notes_set: LOST the June 2, 2026 NJ-12 Democratic primary (outside top 3, 13-way field; Adam Hamawy won; seat open after Watson Coleman retirement). Former U.S. DOE clean-energy policy advisor. Documented positions (own campaign Issues page / WHYY): 'reproductive care' as a human right (pro-choice); 'LGBTQ+ rights are human rights'; 'abolish ICE,' end immigration raids; strict gun control ('weapons of war have no place on our streets or schools'); Medicare for All. No school-choice, religious-liberty, election, or foreign-policy positions located. Status fix is the primary win; positions from cited statements, rest null.

0.note: 'Health care is a human right, and that includes reproductive care'; pro-abortion-rights. Does not affirm personhood from conception.
'LGBTQ+ rights are human rights... [free to love] who they love.' Does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

1.note: Affirms LGBTQ rights including same-sex relationships.

4.note: Champions promotion/protection of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: 'We need to abolish ICE... end Trump's indiscriminate immigration raids'; opposes mandatory deportation.
'Weapons of war have no place on our streets or in our schools'; supports strict gun control / assault-weapons restrictions.
